notes: NT-based S.100 CT resource management platform. Succeeded by IP telephony middleware (SIP, H.323). Eventually subsumed into Intel's acquisition and later Dialogic Group's product portfolio.
Object-oriented C-language media processing API for CT servers. Largely superseded by VoIP/SIP standards in early 2000s.
Windows telephony application programming interface. Remained relevant in enterprise telephony through 2000s; superseded by VoIP APIs.
Hardware bus interoperability standard for CT boards; replaced SC Bus and MVIP. Superseded by packet-switched VoIP architectures.
Time-division multiplexing hardware bus for CT boards. Predated H.100.
Competing CT hardware bus standard. Both MVIP and SC Bus were superseded by H.100.
Telephony Services Application Programming Interface for NetWare. Competed with TAPI. Declined with NetWare market share.
Competitor architecture to CT Media announced June 1997; S.100 support not expected until 1998. Company was acquired by EAS Group in 2005 and folded into Cantata.
S.300 SPI hardware interface and S.400 object-oriented API were proposed extensions to S.100. Never achieved significant adoption before VoIP superseded the ECTF framework.
